message:
feat(frankenpandas): add ExcelReadOptions + read_csv_with_options to prelude (fd90.207 / br-o4ots)

Two related half-exposed-surface defects:

1. ExcelReadOptions is a REQUIRED positional parameter to both
   read_excel(path, &ExcelReadOptions) and read_excel_bytes(data,
   &ExcelReadOptions) — readers that ARE in the prelude. The README
   documents Excel options at line 145 ("sheet_name, has_headers,
   index_col, skip_rows"). User code calling
   `read_excel(path, &ExcelReadOptions::default())` from the prelude
   would not compile because the type wasn't reachable.

2. CsvReadOptions IS in the prelude but read_csv_with_options (the
   only function that consumes it) was NOT. Half the surface — user
   code building a CsvReadOptions then trying to use it would fail
   to resolve the function name.

Fix:
- Add ExcelReadOptions to prelude
- Add read_csv_with_options to prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-nxm3","title":"pandas: DataFrame.apply('prod', axis=1) row-wise parity","description":"DataFrame::apply_named() supports 'prod' for axis=0 only. Add row-wise df.apply('prod', axis=1) parity in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs, including null-skipping numeric behavior and focused regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:24:37.090519634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:04:22.360246932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:04:22.358400422Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-nyc8","title":"Add Series str removeprefix/removesuffix/split_get/join conformance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:13.524023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:15:33.011745662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:15:33.009959391Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-o0x6","title":"fd90.41: SqlReadOptions::coerce_float default should be true to match pandas","description":"Self-review of fd90 commits surfaced a real pandas divergence: SqlReadOptions::coerce_float defaults to false via #[derive(Default)] on pub coerce_float: bool, but pandas defaults to True for pd.read_sql, pd.read_sql_query, and pd.read_sql_table. Per pandas docs: 'coerce_float : bool, default True. Attempts to convert values of non-string, non-numeric objects (like decimal.Decimal) to floating point.' Fix: swap the default by replacing #[derive(Default)] with a manual impl Default for SqlReadOptions that produces coerce_float=true. Existing call sites already pass an explicit value where they care (11 use coerce_float: false explicitly, 13 use true), so the default flip only affects ::default()-constructed instances that didn't explicitly opt in. This is the lowest-impact way to bring the API into pandas parity. Test: add a new test verifying SqlReadOptions::default().coerce_float == true. Update DISCREPANCIES.md to remove any prior note (none exists yet) since we're now matching pandas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:16:13.407693294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:15.981605575Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:15.979049437Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","pandas-parity","review","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-o4ots","title":"fd90.207: Add ExcelReadOptions + read_csv_with_options to prelude","description":"fd90.207: Add ExcelReadOptions + read_csv_with_options to the prelude.

Two related defects in the IO surface:

1. ExcelReadOptions is documented in the README's IO Format Support table at line 145 ("Excel: sheet_name, has_headers, index_col, skip_rows") and is REQUIRED as a positional parameter to both read_excel(path, &ExcelReadOptions) and read_excel_bytes(data, &ExcelReadOptions). User code following the README cannot call either reader without first constructing an ExcelReadOptions, but the type is missing from the prelude.

2. CsvReadOptions IS in the prelude, but the function that consumes it (read_csv_with_options) is NOT. Half the surface — user code with `let opts = CsvReadOptions { ... };` then `read_csv_with_options(text, &opts)` would fail to resolve the function name from the prelude alone.

Scope:
- Add ExcelReadOptions to the prelude
- Add read_csv_with_options to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Both surfaced as half-exposed defects: ExcelReadOptions is a required positional parameter to read_excel/read_excel_bytes (which were in the prelude) but the type was not — user code following README couldn't construct the options. read_csv_with_options consumed CsvReadOptions (which WAS in the prelude) but the function was missing — half the surface. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-o6qo4","title":"fd90.195: Extend categorical integration test to cover all 9 .cat() methods","description":"fd90.195: Extend categorical integration test to cover the remaining .cat() accessor methods (add_categories, remove_unused_categories, set_categories, as_ordered, as_unordered).

The README documents 9 methods on the .cat() accessor (line 422); only 4 are exercised by readme_categorical_analysis (categories, codes, rename_categories, to_values). The other 5 are uncovered.

Scope:
- Extend readme_categorical_analysis_compiles_and_runs (or add a new test) to cover:
  - add_categories(Vec<Scalar>) — extend the category set
  - remove_unused_categories() — drop unobserved categories
  - set_categories(Vec<Scalar>) — replace category set
  - as_ordered() / as_unordered() — toggle ordered flag

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (extend existing test, ~25 lines added).

Now all 9 documented .cat() methods are integration-tested. All 33 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-o8a6","title":"pandas: fp-frame DataFrame.pivot_table dropna=False parity","description":"py signature:
df.pivot_table(values='v', index='a', columns='b', aggfunc='sum', dropna=False) -> DataFrame

Current pivot_table silently drops index/column keys whose values are NaN when building group cells (scalar_key_allow_missing buckets them, but the caller can't opt out of pandas' default dropna=True or force dropna=False to keep all NaN combinations). Add pivot_table_dropna(values, index, columns, aggfunc, dropna: bool): when dropna=false, null index/column keys become an explicit category row/column with NaN aggregation output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:51:09.576418912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:24:31.084040002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:24:31.081972277Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
